2605.24057.title: Feature Lottery? A Bifurcation Theory of Concept Emergence
2605.24057.paper_summary: Neural networks acquire structured representations at specific moments during training, yet identifying these transitions typically relies on retrospective, label-dependent metrics. We introduce a bifurcation theory of representation dynamics to detect these moments in real time. Analyzing a passive GMM probe attached to the evolving encoder, we show the onset of structure corresponds to a supercritical pitchfork bifurcation driven by the loss Hessian. The system exhibits a theoretically predictable zero-crossing ($β_c$) that, compared to the network's current state ($β$), yields a dynamic ratio $β(t)/β_c(t)$: a universal, label-free phase coordinate for representation dynamics, computable entirely from hidden states. We empirically validate four distinct transition regimes predicted by this coordinate across diverse settings: SAEs on language models (Pythia), SSL (CIFAR), and grokking (modular arithmetic). Crucially, under finite dissipation, macroscopic symmetry-breaking can lag the initial zero-crossing by orders of magnitude, which providing a rigorous dynamical account of the delayed escape observed in grokking. Microscopically, the bifurcation creates a shared unstable subspace, forcing collective symmetry breaking. We term this the "feature lottery" in SAE training: a feature's terminal interpretability becomes predictable remarkably early. By only 5% of training, early atom purity robustly predicts final convergence purity, with top-decile early atoms achieving over 12x the baseline purity at convergence. Beyond explaining concept emergence, $β/β_c$ provides a practical early-warning indicator for training health, detecting the onset of usable structure, the crystallization of feature identity, and representational collapse epochs before downstream metrics react.
2605.24057.paper_summary_zh: 神經網絡在訓練過程中的特定時刻獲得結構化表示，但識別這些轉變通常依賴於回顧性、依賴標籤的指標。<lb/>我們引入了一種表示動態的分岔理論，以實時檢測這些時刻。<lb/>分析附加在不斷演變的編碼器上的被動 GMM 探針，我們顯示結構的出現對應於由損失 Hessian 驅動的超臨界叉形分岔。<lb/>該系統顯示出理論上可預測的零交叉 ($β_c$)，與網絡當前狀態 ($β$) 相比，產生動態比率 $β(t)/β_c(t)$：一個通用的、無標籤的表示動態相坐標，完全可以從隱藏狀態計算得出。<lb/>我們在不同設定中實證驗證了這個坐標預測的四種不同轉變範疇：語言模型上的 SAE（Pythia）、SSL（CIFAR）和 grokking（模塊算術）。<lb/>關鍵是，在有限耗散下，宏觀對稱破缺可能會滯後於初始零交叉數量級，這為觀察到的 grokking 延遲逃逸提供了一個嚴謹的動態解釋。<lb/>在微觀層面上，分岔創造了一個共享的不穩定子空間，迫使集體對稱破缺。<lb/>我們將這稱為 SAE 訓練中的「特徵彩票」：一個特徵的終端可解釋性變得可預測得相當早。<lb/>在訓練的前 5% 時期，早期原子純度穩健地預測最終收斂純度，前十分之一的早期原子在收斂時達到基線純度的 12 倍以上。<lb/>除了解釋概念的出現，$β/β_c$ 還提供了一個實用的早期預警指標，用於訓練健康，檢測可用結構的出現、特徵身份的結晶以及表示崩潰的時期，早於下游指標的反應。
2605.24057.author: Fuming Yang et.al.
